mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) or produce negative returns during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override earnings growth, causing investors to de-risk equity portfolios and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, which accelerates revenue growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or forward 60-day returns are negative when both RSAFS and HOUST rise concurrently for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained inflation periods, declines in the industrial production index signal capacity bottlenecks that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, triggering immediate equity sell-offs over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns do not turn negative following INDPRO declines when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or the mechanism shows zero predictive accuracy in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4% forces markets to price in prolonged monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 70% of instances where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 55% of observations, or interaction terms show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in predictive regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding economic output that directly boosts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or the signal produces negative alpha after transaction costs in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production surpasses 104.0, manufacturing capacity utilization reaches levels that support strong corporate earnings growth, overcoming inflation concerns and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in over 60% of months where INDPRO exceeds 104.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS levels.
